summary:
Vertical lines in distal esophageal mucosa (VLEM) are often considered part of an inflammatory pattern but have no proven diagnostic value when present without other signs of disease or histological confirmation. They should be interpreted within context; their presence does not confirm esophagitis independently.

Final Answer: {B}

The summary focuses only on clinically significant findings related directly to diagnosing or differentiating esophagitis versus non-inflammation in pediatrics — avoiding general statements about imaging, anatomy, or unrelated topics.
